info.title: EthisysCore CLI
info.description: CLI tool for scaffolding, packaging, and running EthisysCore plugins.

commands.name: build-image
commands.description: Build a Docker container image for a container plugin

options.name: --image
options.description:
Override the image name/tag from manifest
(e.g. myregistry.azurecr.io/plugins/movies:1.0.0)

options.name: --skip-build
options.description:
Skip 'dotnet build' (use if already built)
Prerequisites:
- Docker must be running
- Plugin must have ExecutionMode = Container in its manifest
- Plugin .csproj must include <PublishProfile>DefaultContainer</PublishProfile>

commands.name: Flags
commands.description:
--version, -v         Print the CLI version
--internal            Use ProjectReference to EthisysCore.Plugin.Sdk (for developers
working inside the EthisysCore repo). This is the default.
Omit this flag for external clients who use the NuGet package.
--template            UI framework template: html, react, or vue
--mode                Execution mode: in-process (default), out-of-process, or container
--layered             Use 4-project Clean Architecture (Domain, Application, Infrastructure, Plugin)

commands.name: from-schema
commands.description: Generate a compiled plugin from a DynamicApp JSON schema

commands.name: generate-manifest
commands.description: Extract manifest from compiled DLL and write extension.manifest.json

options.name: --output
options.description:
Output path for extension.manifest.json
(default: <build-output>/extension.manifest.json)

options.name: --overlay
options.description:
Optional overlay JSON for UI contributions and
other metadata not in the SDK PluginManifest

options.name: --headless
options.description: Suppress warning for missing UI contributions

commands.name: package
commands.description: Build, bundle, and sign a .ccpkg package (auto-builds Docker image for container plugins)

options.name: --output
options.description: Output path for the .ccpkg file

options.name: --key-dir
options.description: Directory for RSA key pair (default: <path>)

options.name: --skip-ui
options.description: Skip building and bundling the frontend UI

options.name: --skip-image
options.description: Skip Docker image building for container plugins

options.name: --image
options.description: Override the container image name/tag from manifest

options.name: --headless
options.description:
Package a backend-only extension without UI contributions
Signing:
All plugins share a single RSA-2048 key pair. The private key signs
packages; the matching public key is embedded inside the .ccpkg.
The server verifies signatures using the embedded key and stores it
per-extension on first install (Trust On First Use). Subsequent
upgrades must be signed with the same key.

commands.name: sandbox
commands.description: Launch the local development environment (Aspire)

options.name: --version

options.name: --internal
options.description:

working inside the EthisysCore repo). This is the default.
Omit this flag for external clients who use the NuGet package.

options.description:
Use ProjectReference to EthisysCore.Plugin.Sdk (for developers
working inside the EthisysCore repo). This is the default.
Omit this flag for external clients who use the NuGet package.

options.name: --template
options.description: UI framework template: html, react, or vue

options.name: --mode
options.description: Execution mode: in-process (default), out-of-process, or container

options.name: --layered
options.description: Use 4-project Clean Architecture (Domain, Application, Infrastructure, Plugin)
